findings.detail: All user input (conviction text, confirmation responses) sanitized via sanitize_for_llm_prompt() before archiving. Input also runs through regex parsers (_parse_conviction, _parse_outcome) that constrain extraction to expected patterns.

_DEBIASING_NOTICE communicates AI overconfidence bias (systematic ~15% in 0.75-0.85 confidence range) to user BEFORE showing AI analysis. Conviction-first design: user states independent conviction before seeing any AI results (CONVICTION_FIRST → ANALYSE → CONFIRMING). This prevents anchoring.

All conversation turns logged via archiver.log_turn() with sanitized text. No raw user input stored unsanitized. Decision data (direction, conviction values, outcome) archived as structured dict — no PII present.
Session data contains only session_id (generated identifier), direction, conviction numbers (1-10), and outcome (CONTINUE/MODIFY/PAUSE). No personal identifiers, account numbers, or credentials.
datetime.now(timezone.utc) used at line 71 (Gate2Session.started_at). No bare datetime.now() calls.
No subprocess calls, no shell execution, no eval/exec. All string operations are pure Python regex matching and formatting.
Red Team survivors displayed with severity-filtered presentation (critical/major only). Challenge text truncated to 200 chars. No raw red team data exposed unsanitized.
